reasoning: Stock prices generally follow a near random walk in the short term, with a slight upward drift leading to a base rate of roughly 53-54% for a positive one-week return. Over the last month, KKR has experienced a strong downward trend, falling from over $130 in December to $85.93 on March 13. However, negative momentum is often offset by mean reversion and short-term bounces, typical over a one-week horizon, and a recent insider purchase of 50,000 shares by the Co-CEO at an average price of $87.81 has provided moderate bullish signal that near-term downside may be limited. Moreover, statistical modeling of KKR's one-week prior performance and seasonality metrics point to roughly a 0.57 expected probability of an increase. Balancing the historical/seasonal base rates with the recent intense downward momentum and near-term market conditions post-earnings, I estimate roughly a 55% probability that the price on March 20 (the closest trading day to March 22) will close higher than $85.93.
